name: Solid-State Thermal Capacitance for Passive Cold-Chain
mechanism: Phase-Change Material (Salt-Hydrate PCM) + Vacuum Insulation Panels
loss_model: Conduction through VIP (k~0.004 W/mK), Radiation (emissivity-dependent), Infiltration (seal leakage)
regime: Steady-state heat transfer with phase-change energy storage
boundary: Insulated container, external ambient 20-40°C, internal target -20°C
performance_claim: Maintain -20°C for 30 days with ZERO external power
value_proposition: Prevents $35B annual cold-chain failures (insulin, vaccines, biologics). Eliminates diesel generator dependency. Target: pharmaceutical logistics with premium cold-window insurance.
bom: PCM material: $500-2000/container, VIP: $1000-3000/container, thermoelectric harvest: $500 (optional). Total BOM: $2k-$5.5k per container.
profit_model: Two-tier: (1) Cold-Chain Audit License - certify routes for passive cooling ($50k-$200k per shipping company), (2) SSTC liner sales/rental - $10k-$20k per container
market_timing: Pharmaceutical cold-chain growth 8-10% CAGR. Regulatory pressure on carbon footprint (diesel reefers). Cold-chain failure insurance market expanding.
market_description: Global cold-chain logistics: $250B market. Pharmaceutical segment: $18B, 8% CAGR. Reefer container fleet: 3.5M units globally. Target: 1% penetration = 35,000 units.
hardware_requirement: Custom container liner (retrofit existing reefers). PCM charging infrastructure at port facilities.
assumptions: 30-day duration assumes perfect seals (no air leakage)
Diesel reefer fuel cost: $3000-5000 per 30-day voyage
Cold-chain failure rate: 5-15% of shipments (WHO data)
loss_closure: Fourier conduction (VIP), Stefan-Boltzmann radiation (emissivity 0.05-0.1), Infiltration heat load (seal quality-dependent). Phase-change energy storage: Q = m*L_f where L_f = latent heat of fusion.
failure_modes: PCM degradation after 100+ cycles (reduced latent heat)
Seal failure (air infiltration dominates heat load)

hostile_questions.question: Is '30 days at -20°C' based on actual container testing or theoretical calculation?
acceptable: Lab-scale prototype tested, full-scale validation needed
Thermal model validated against industry VIP data, container test in progress
Conservative estimate: 30 days assumes perfect seals, real-world 20-25 days
forbidden: PCM spec sheets say it works

hostile_questions.question: What if VIP thermal conductivity degrades to 0.010 W/mK (puncture, aging)?
acceptable: Performance drops to 12-15 days. Mitigation: redundant VIP layers

hostile_questions.question: What if PCM + VIP liner costs $8k but only saves $3k in diesel per voyage?
acceptable: Payback time: 2.7 voyages (6-8 months for weekly routes)
Value proposition shifts to failure insurance, not fuel savings
Target high-value pharma cargo where failure cost >> fuel cost
